title: Pilot brief + derivative dataset, end-to-end (low-controversy indicator)
context: The M0 milestone must prove the full end-to-end pipeline on one low-controversy, structural indicator (e.g. global/regional food supply in kcal/capita/day or cereal production trends). This pilot exercises every gate — licence, statistical integrity, non-alarmist editorial, and reproducibility — and delivers a real output to a beneficiary via an informal channel or Zenodo/open-repo self-serve fallback. It is the proof-of-concept that every subsequent brief/dataset task builds on.
objective: Produce and deliver one pilot derivative dataset and plain-language brief for a low-controversy FAOSTAT indicator, passing all three review gates and delivering the output to a named beneficiary or self-serve channel with acceptance evidence recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: Pilot indicator is structural, long-run, and low-controversy (e.g. food supply kcal/capita/day or cereal production trends), selected from the TOPIC-CATALOG.md shortlist with a realistic delivery path.

gate-004 PASS artifact committed: CC-BY-IGO confirmed, permitsDerivatives: true with cited clause/URL, attribution string + non-endorsement disclaimer recorded, PII screen passes (aggregate-only), sensitivity screen passes (no acute-crisis framing).

Derivative dataset built by the deterministic harness: tidy long-format CSV, datapackage.json (Frictionless), and Croissant ML JSON-LD metadata; status flags decoded; published uncertainty preserved; source totals reconciled within documented tolerance; output checksum committed and re-derived in CI.

Plain-language brief passes the statistical-integrity checklist (every figure resolves to a committed dataset row), passes the non-alarmist editorial review (style-guide checklist artifact committed, zero unresolved flags), includes indicator definition, reference period, status-flag disclosure, uncertainty shown, 'what the data does not say' section, 'not advice' notice, FAO attribution + non-endorsement disclaimer, and meets the readability target.
All three gate sign-offs committed: domain-expert (statistical correctness), editorial (non-alarmist standard), and licence/provenance (attribution + reproducibility).

Output delivered to and accepted/used by a named beneficiary (informal channel) or submitted to Zenodo/open repo (self-serve fallback) with the Steward's acceptance-evidence artifact (outcomes/<topic-id>.json) recorded — or submitted with the specific blocker surfaced.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; commit is DCO signed-off.
output: A committed derivative dataset (CSV + datapackage.json + Croissant metadata) and plain-language brief for the pilot indicator, with all review artifacts and acceptance-evidence artifact recorded in outcomes/<topic-id>.json.
